messages.content: These risk write-ups share funnel target nodes, which does NOT make them the same risk — a funnel is coarse, so mechanistically distinct risks (a certification clock, a grid-connection queue, a distributor line-review cycle) can all compile to the same node. PARTITION the cluster into groups of TRUE duplicates: same underlying mechanism, same causal story, such that presenting both to a client would read as repetition. Distinct risks come back as singleton groups (absorbs: []). For a genuine duplicate group, pick the best exemplar (sharpest mechanism, best evidence) as keepId and fold the absorbed write-ups' UNIQUE early-warning indicators into it. Over-merging destroys distinct insights — when in doubt, keep them separate.

content: {"rationale": "All three write-ups describe the identical mechanism: global framework agreements and integrated rack/power/cooling bundles foreclose the 0.40 large-operator cell that the venture's GTM targets first. Same causal story (frameworks decided globally, not open tender), same evidence (Digital Realty-Schneider, Vertiv house accounts, unsourced 0.40 estimate, 30%-of-orders datapoint), same model impact (~half the cell non-contestable in Year 1). These are true duplicates. The ledger-self-audit version (judgeTotal 17) has the sharpest mechanism, ties to serviceableFactor/obtainableFactor, and adds a unique AVL-opening indicator, so it serves as keepId.", "groups": [{"keepId": "risk.ledger-self-audit.framework-agreements-hollow-large-operator-cell", "absorbs": ["risk.base-rate-analogy.framework-foreclosure-beachhead", "risk.execution-window.framework-agreements-foreclose-large-operator-cell"], "foldedIndicators": [{"signal": "Bidder counts and substitution clauses on standalone rack-PDU tenders", "where": "TED (Tenders Electronic Daily) award notices and operator supplier portals", "threshold": "Median bidders \\u22642 or 'no substitution' language on >half of observed PDU line items", "updates": "increases"}]}]}
